skill.name: Page Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO)
skill.description: Analyze and optimize individual pages for conversion performance.
skill.summary: Expert framework for diagnosing page conversion issues and providing evidence-based optimization recommendations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code, network requests, or system access. Static analysis correctly identified zero security risks (0/100). The skill provides a structured CRO framework through text prompts only. No malicious patterns, injection attempts, or data exfiltration vectors detected.

content.value_statement: Many pages fail to convert because of unclear messaging and poor user experience. This skill provides a structured framework to diagnose conversion barriers and prioritize high-impact improvements.
actual_capabilities: Calculate Page Conversion Readiness Index score (0-100) across six weighted categories
Diagnose value proposition clarity and headline effectiveness
Evaluate conversion goal focus and CTA hierarchy
Assess traffic-message match and trust signal placement
Identify friction points and UX barriers affecting conversions
Generate prioritized recommendations with testable hypotheses
limitations: Cannot access real-time analytics or heatmaps without user-provided data
Cannot execute A/B tests or implement changes directly
Relies on user accuracy when describing current page state and traffic sources

use_cases.title: Landing Page Optimization
use_cases.description: Diagnose why a paid traffic landing page has high bounce rates and low conversion. Get prioritized fixes for message match and CTA clarity.
use_cases.target_user: Digital marketing managers running paid campaigns

use_cases.title: Homepage Conversion Audit
use_cases.description: Evaluate homepage effectiveness for visitor routing and positioning clarity. Identify quick wins and structural improvements.
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders optimizing their primary web presence

use_cases.title: Pricing Page Improvement
use_cases.description: Analyze pricing page friction, objection handling, and risk reduction. Get copy alternatives and trust signal recommendations.
use_cases.target_user: SaaS product managers preparing for a pricing redesign

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this [page type] for conversion readiness. Here is the current content: [paste page content]. Calculate the Readiness Index score and identify the top 3 issues holding back conversions.

prompt_templates.title: Headline and CTA Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my headline and primary CTA for clarity and action-orientation. Current headline: [X]. Current CTA: [Y]. Target audience: [describe]. Provide 3 alternative versions with rationale.

prompt_templates.title: Objection Handling Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: I sell [product/service] to [audience]. List the 5 most likely objections visitors have on my [page type]. For each, suggest specific copy or elements that address the concern.

prompt_templates.title: Full CRO Diagnostic with Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a complete CRO audit of my [page type]. Page URL/content: [paste]. Traffic source: [describe]. Current conversion rate: [X%]. Provide Readiness Index score, prioritized recommendations, and 3 testable hypotheses with success metrics.

output_examples.input: Landing page for project management software, 2.1% conversion rate from paid search
output_examples.output: Readiness Index: 58/100 (Low Readiness). Top issues: Value proposition buried below fold, no social proof near CTA, form has 8 fields causing friction. Quick win: Move testimonial block above CTA and reduce form to 4 fields.

output_examples.input: Pricing page with high drop-off at trial signup
output_examples.output: Readiness Index: 72/100 (Moderate Readiness). Key blocker: Unclear difference between plans. Recommendation: Add feature comparison table, move FAQ section closer to pricing cards, add guarantee badge near signup button.

best_practices: Always calculate the Readiness Index before recommending tests - scores below 70 indicate foundational issues that testing cannot fix
Prioritize recommendations by impact and effort - address high-confidence quick wins before structural changes
Map every recommendation to a specific scoring category and conversion constraint for clarity
anti_patterns: Making cosmetic changes without diagnosing root conversion barriers
Running A/B tests on pages with unclear value propositions or ambiguous goals
Optimizing for vanity metrics instead of primary conversion actions

faq.question: What traffic level do I need for this skill to be useful?
faq.answer: This skill works at any traffic level for diagnostic purposes. However, A/B testing recommendations require sufficient traffic for statistical significance - typically 1000+ visitors per variant per month.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze my page if I only provide the URL?
faq.answer: For best results, provide page content directly. The skill cannot access external URLs. Copy and paste your page content, or describe key elements like headlines, CTAs, and layout.

faq.question: How accurate is the Readiness Index score?
faq.answer: The index is a diagnostic framework, not a predictive model. Accuracy depends on honest self-assessment of each category. Use it to identify gaps, not as an absolute success metric.

faq.question: Should I implement all recommendations at once?
faq.answer: No. Start with quick wins that require minimal effort. Then prioritize high-impact changes. Test structural changes before cosmetic ones to validate their effect.

faq.question: Does this skill work for B2B and B2C pages?
faq.answer: Yes. The framework applies to both, though B2B pages typically score lower on trust signals and objection handling due to longer sales cycles and higher stakes.

faq.question: What if my page already has a high conversion rate?
faq.answer: High-converting pages can still benefit. The skill identifies optimization opportunities you may have missed and helps maintain performance as traffic sources or audiences change.
